title: cuscuta racemosa var. racemosa
intro: Cuscuta racemosa var. racemosa, also known as the woodland dodder, is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to and siphoning off nutrients from surrounding plants, typically through its tendrils. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Cuscuta racemosa var. racemosa are said to include reduced blood pressure, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced immune function. Cuscuta racemosa var. racemosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to utilize its medicinal properties.
